steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content:
The blank follows “acknowledge the” and precedes “do so breaks”.
Left side: “actors typically won’t acknowledge the ___” is a complete independent clause once a noun fills the blank.
Right side: “___ do so breaks the fourth wall” becomes “To do so breaks the fourth wall” — an infinitive phrase acting as subject, also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the boundary rule
steps.content:
A period (or semicolon) separates two independent clauses.
Choice C gives “audience. To” which supplies the needed period.
The other three either omit punctuation, use a comma splice, or add ungrammatical wording, breaking the clause boundary.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
